_comment: Pages the pre-gate must NOT wrongly call single-column. arXiv two-column is already covered by reading_order_corpus.json; these cover non-academic layouts the heuristic was never tuned on.

must_not_short_circuit.notes: CDC MMWR Vol 73 Issue 39 (Oct 2024). Classic two-column government medical report produced with Adobe InDesign. Page 2 has 36 text blocks split left/right with column x-midpoints near 36-296 (left col) and 305-576 (right col); wide-block fraction is 2/36 = 0.056, well below the 0.6 majority threshold.

IRS Internal Revenue Bulletin 2024-01. Classic two-column government bulletin. Page 2 has 16 text blocks split left/right; blocks span x0~30-285 (left col) and x0~306-561 (right col); wide-block fraction is 2/16 = 0.125, well below threshold.
CDC VitalSigns May 2018 infographic-style two-column report. Page 4 has 24 text blocks arranged in two columns; wide-block fraction is 0/24 = 0.0, well below threshold.

cjk_horizontal_classify.notes: Japan Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications (総務省) 2024 Information and Communication White Paper summary, landscape A4 (780x540 pt). Page 2 is a Japanese-language (CJK horizontal) single-column table-of-contents page; 8 text blocks all spanning roughly the full text width (750 pt); wide-block fraction 5/8 = 0.625, above the 0.6 threshold => correctly classified as single-column.
